transcript.text: What does this project do, and who uses it?
Browser SDK for an AI teaching avatar, used by edtech customers to embed a conversational avatar directly into their own lesson pages.
What's the deployment model, and where does this actually run once it's on the customer's page?
It loads via `<script src>` from our CDN or `npm install` directly into the customer's page — browser-direct-js, no iframe, no sandbox. Same as the README shows.
Who's the audience, and are there any compliance or regulatory requirements to account for?
Enterprise edtech customers embed this in their own lesson pages. No SOC2/HIPAA/PCI requirement today — no payment or health data ever touches this SDK.
Is this project AI-powered, or built with, or used by, LLM coding agents?

It's built with Claude Code day to day, but the SDK package itself doesn't call an LLM at runtime — it mounts and tears down the avatar widget and streams the mic when enabled. The actual conversational model runs on our backend, outside this package.
I see a getUserMedia call gated behind enableMicrophone — is microphone access required or optional, and what's the Permissions-Policy expectation?
Optional. Off by default. Lessons with voice commands set `enableMicrophone: true`, and the host page has to grant `Permissions-Policy: microphone=(self)` for it to work.
What minimum percentage of the codebase needs to be exercised before you're satisfied — what bar should Karen enforce?
80%, matching what CLAUDE.md already says as the stopping criterion.

permissions.microphone: Optional — voice-driven lessons only, off by default; customer opts in via enableMicrophone and grants Permissions-Policy: microphone=(self)

exceedsBaseline.note: Zero runtime dependencies — package.json's dependencies field is empty; the entire SDK is built on browser platform APIs alone

gateScripts.gate-1-supply-chain:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
cd "$root"
issues=0
out=""
if [ -f package.json ]; then
  audit_json="$(npm audit --omit=dev --json 2>/dev/null || true)"
  vulns="$(echo "$audit_json" | node -e "let d='';process.stdin.on('data',c=>d+=c);process.stdin.on('end',()=>{try{const j=JSON.parse(d);console.log(j.metadata?.vulnerabilities?.total||0)}catch(e){console.log(0)}})")"
  if [ "$vulns" -gt 0 ]; then
    issues=$vulns
    out="package.json:1\t$vulns known vulnerable dependenc$([ "$vulns" = 1 ] && echo y || echo ies) via npm audit"$'\n'
  fi
fi
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-2-completeness:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
cd "$root"
issues=0
out=""
while IFS= read -r hit; do
  file="${hit%%:*}"
  rest="${hit#*:}"
  line="${rest%%:*}"
  out+="$file:$line\tstub or TODO left in shipped source"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
done < <(grep -rnE 'TODO|FIXME|not implemented' src/ 2>/dev/null || true)
npx --no-install eslint src/ --format unix >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-3-security:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
cd "$root"
issues=0
out=""
# global-scope-write: assignment to window.*/globalThis.* outside constructor opt-in
while IFS= read -r hit; do
  file="${hit%%:*}"; line="${hit#*:}"; line="${line%%:*}"
  out+="$file:$line\tglobal write to window/globalThis outside constructor opt-in config"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
done < <(node tools/scan-global-writes.mjs src/ 2>/dev/null || true)
# unremoved-listener, hardcoded-secret, credentials-include, eval-in-comment,
# password-variable, console-debug-guard all use the same structural AST scan
node tools/scan-security-rules.mjs src/ --format=gate >> /tmp/gate3.$$ 2>/dev/null || true
while IFS= read -r line; do
  [ -z "$line" ] && continue
  out+="$line"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
done < /tmp/gate3.$$ 2>/dev/null || true
rm -f /tmp/gate3.$$
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; echo "ZERO-TOLERANCE"; fi
[ "$issues" -eq 0 ] && exit 0 || exit 1
gateScripts.gate-4-docs-parity:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
cd "$root"
issues=0
out=""
while IFS= read -r hit; do
  out+="$hit\tREADME.md code block failed to execute against current exports"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
done < <(node tools/doctest.mjs README.md --languages=js,ts --annotation=karen:runnable 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^FAIL' || true)
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-6-test-integrity:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
cd "$root"
issues=0
out=""
npx --no-install vitest run --coverage --reporter=json > /tmp/vitest.$$ 2>/dev/null || true
pct="$(node -e "try{const r=require('/tmp/vitest.\$\$');console.log(Math.round((r.coverageMap?.total?.lines?.pct)||100))}catch(e){console.log(100)}" 2>/dev/null || echo 100)"
rm -f /tmp/vitest.$$
if [ "${pct:-100}" -lt 80 ]; then
  out+="coverage/lcov.info:1\tline coverage $pct% below the 80% threshold in .karen.json"$'\n'
  issues=1
fi
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-7-agent-context:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
cd "$root"
issues=0
out=""
for f in CLAUDE.md; do
  if [ ! -f "$f" ]; then
    out+="$f:0\tagent context file missing from repo root"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
    continue
  fi
  grep -q "Stopping criteria" "$f" || { out+="$f:1\tmissing a stopping-criteria section"$'\n'; issues=$((issues+1)); }
done
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
